items.title: Het kan verkeren bij FC Utrecht: 'Europees voetbal is weer realistisch'
items.summary: Ron Jans durft weer van Europees voetbal te dromen met FC Utrecht. Lange tijd moest de 67-jarige trainer naar beneden kijken met zijn club, maar na de zege op AZ ziet de wereld er ineens een stuk anders uit.

items.title: VI Live: Malen scoort ook tegen Juventus, Paixão maakt heerlijke goal tegen Lyon
items.summary: Zondag staan er liefst vijf Eredivisie-duels op de rol. Ajax en Feyenoord hebben een apart liveblog. Hier volg je onder meer de ontmoeting tussen FC Utrecht en AZ. Arsenal ontvangt Chelsea en in Italië staan AS Roma en Juventus tegenover elkaar. Volg het in de VI Live.

items.title: Na vijftig vruchteloze pogingen is het raak: Sondre Ørjasaeter over zijn bevrijdende goal
items.summary: Het was de zondag van de ontluikende lente in Twente. Met een thuisploeg die overtuigend afrekende met Feyenoord en eindelijk weer van een topclub won, een verdediging die opnieuw de nul hield én, zowaar, een doelpunt van Sondre Ørjasaeter. De Noor verhaalt over zijn verlossing.

items.title: Mikel Arteta praises David Raya for vital saves in ‘heartstopping’ Arsenal victory
items.summary: Goalkeeper made last-ditch save to deny Garnacho ‘David’s hand brought my heart back to life’ Mikel Arteta said his heart almost stopped before David Raya kept ­Arsenal’s title challenge on track with a stunning late save to deny 10‑man Chelsea a late equaliser at the Emirates Stadium. Raya was in exceptional form ­during a crucial 2-1 win, making a series of excellent stops during a tense encounter, and the Spaniard was alert when a cross from Alejandro Garnacho threatened to drift inside his near post as full time approached. The goalkeeper pushed the ball wide, ensuring that goals from William Saliba and Jurriën Timber enabled Arsenal to restore their five-point lead over Manchester City at the top of the Premier League. Continue reading...

items.title: Gelukkig Go Ahead Eagles wint ook op bezoek bij Excelsior
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles heeft voor de tweede week op rij gewonnen. Na de overtuigende zege op Heracles Almelo was het zondagavond op bezoek bij Excelsior veel minder fraai: de Rotterdammers waren sterker, maar trokken uiteindelijk aan het kortste eind.

items.title: Ajax is geen centimeter opgeschoten onder Fred Grim
items.summary: Ajax wil naar de Champions League, maar solliciteerde zondagmiddag eerder naar een plek in de kelderklasse. In de uitwedstrijd tegen PEC Zwolle (0-0) zakte de ploeg van Fred Grim voetballend flink door de ondergrens. 'Dan mag je wel in de spiegel kijken', vindt de trainer. Dat levert een pijnlijk beeld op.

items.title: Iran federation says participation in WC in doubt
items.summary: The president of Iran's soccer federation says he does not know if the national team can play World Cup matches in the United States following the surprise U.S. and Israeli bombardment of his country.

items.title: Arteta: 'Heart almost stopped' on late Raya save
items.summary: Mikel Arteta hailed David Raya's performance in Arsenal's 2-1 win against Chelsea after saying his "heart almost stopped" before the goalkeeper made a crucial stoppage time save to keep the Gunners in pole position in the Premier League title race.

items.title: United got 'Old Trafford bonus' in win - Palace boss
items.summary: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner has branded the decision to award Manchester United a penalty in their 2-1 defeat on Sunday an "Old Trafford bonus."

items.title: Infantino calls for red cards for covering mouths
items.summary: UEFA president Gianni Infantino has called for players who cover their mouth in on-pitch confrontations to be sent off.

items.title: Arsenal beat 10-man Chelsea to extend lead
items.summary: Arsenal get an important win in the title race against Chelsea, who had Pedro Neto sent off for a second yellow in a game with three goals scored from corners.

items.title: Sesko leads Man Utd to victory against 10-man Crystal Palace
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko scores his sixth goal in eight games to help Manchester United secure a comeback victory against Crystal Palace and move up to third in the Premier League.

items.title: Fulham sink struggling Spurs with incredible Iwobi goal
items.summary: Alex Iwobi scores a beautifully-placed goal as Fulham hold off late pressure from Tottenham Hotspur to win 2-1.

items.title: Forest fall to defeat at Brighton despite Gibbs White stunner
items.summary: Brighton beat Nottingham Forest as Danny Welbeck scored his 10th goal of the season, to claim back-to-back wins for the first time since November.

items.title: Iranian football association unsure if national team will play at World Cup in US
items.summary: ‘We cannot look forward with hope,’ says FFIRI president England Lions match in Abu Dhabi cancelled due to war The president of Iran’s football federation says he does not know if the national team can play World Cup matches in the US after the US and Israeli bombardment of the country. “What is certain is that after this attack, we cannot be expected to look forward to the World Cup with hope,” Mehdi Taj said to the sports portal Varzesh3 as Iran traded strikes with Israel as part of a widening war prompted by the bombardment. Continue reading...

items.title: Nev: Arsenal face '10 more weeks of pain - but they will get there'
items.summary: Gary Neville thinks Arsenal will still edge Manchester City to the Premier League title but warned Gunners fans they face "10 weeks of pain" en route to their first title in 22 years.

items.title: Arsenal riding corner kicks to Premier League title; Chelsea's red card problem continues
items.summary: Arsenal may be the most dominant corner kick team the Premier League has ever seen. Meanwhile, Chelsea keep hurting themselves with red cards.

items.title: Tudor fury at 'home referee' as Spurs left in 'big emergency'
items.summary: Without a win in 10 Premier League matches – their worst league run in 22 years – Tottenham are facing a battle to stay up.

items.title: Corner frenzy! Arsenal restore five-point gap at the top with win over 10-man Chelsea
items.summary: FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Arsenal’s match against Chelsea in the Premier League.

items.title: Tottenham lacking in attack, midfield, defence and ‘brain’, says Igor Tudor
items.summary: 2-1 defeat at Fulham was Spurs’ fourth league loss in a row ‘We are always late on everything. That’s the problem’ Igor Tudor described the situation Tottenham find themselves in as “amazing” and suggested they have just three major problems as they fight relegation: the attack, the midfield and the defence. Spurs’ 2-1 defeat at Fulham was their fourth in a row in the league and leaves them four points above the relegation zone. “I cannot tell you anything new,” said a downbeat Tudor. “We need to find the forces inside each of us. I said to the players: ‘It’s always what you’re going to do, what you want to do with yourself,’ you know? More personality, more wish to do before reacting, plenty of things … We are lacking when we attack, we lack the quality to score the goal. We are lacking in the middle to run and we are lacking behind to stay there to suffer and not concede the goal. So, an amazing situation. Amazing.” Continue reading...
